label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED REAR with mid-band HALL — side-split: bedrooms in left column organised as two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns, LDK in right column (great_room front, kitchen rear). Left sub-column: [master, ensuite]. Mid sub-column: [standard, hall, common T&B]. Both baths cluster at the rear sharing a wet-stack plumbing wall with the kitchen. Compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld -- use this gr (combined great_room) form down to ~5 m buildable depth; switch to the ld (living/dining split) form once buildable depth reaches ~10 m lot depth (6 m envelope) or more.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's rear (north) wall opens north into ensuite. Ensuite sits at the rear-left corner behind master (canonical kitchen-right form: master at left, ensuite directly behind).
master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + 2x 0.1 m frame stops. This is master's only public access.
standard's north wall has a door opening north into the hall — 1.0 m floor. Standard has no other public access.
common bath's south wall has a door opening south into the hall — the bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the common bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group.
common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to hall on compact lots.
common bath and ensuite share a plumbing wall — common's west meets ensuite's east, completing the 3-room wet-stack cluster at the rear (ensuite + common + kitchen, all rear-band).

hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (no door — codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open-plan). 0.9 m min mouth — looser than the 1.0 m used elsewhere in the catalog because the wide-shell side-split's stacked-column geometry forces a tight rear-band budget on compact lots. On 14x10 with kitchen pinned at 1.8 m depth and hall at 1.2 m depth, allowing 0.9 m here lets common bath cap at 2.8 × 1.5 (= 4.2 sqm). The 0.9 m mouth is still a legal PH door-clear opening for single-person flow — two-person flow loses, but a starter home rarely needs it.

hall's east wall continues to face the kitchen for the small strip where the hall depth exceeds great_room depth (on compact lots, hall.y_end is at the bath block's south edge while great.y_end is at the kitchen's north edge, and the two y values differ by ~0.3 m when kitchen is pinned at 1.8 m). Declaring this as open_plan tells the renderer to suppress the wall there — LDK is conceptually one open space, so the hall opens onto great AND kitchen as a continuous mouth, not a mouth into great with a 0.3 m wall sliver between hall and kitchen. Min 0.1 m so this adjacency holds even when the strip is minimal.
great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) stacked front-to-rear in the right column.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_side_split_rear_baths
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: On compact wide lots (<75 sqm buildable, e.g. 14x10 m = 60 sqm), kitchen is floored at 1.6 m depth (down from an original 1.8 m -- see 2026-07-20 update below) and hall's greatest-dimension is now a 2.2-2.8 m RANGE (was pinned exactly at 2.8 m). Both baths are PINNED at 2.8 × 1.5 m = 4.2 sqm (max_least 1.5, max_greatest 2.8, max_area 4.2). This makes ensuite and common identical in shape, so the wall separating them on the column divider (x = master_east = standard_west) starts cleanly at the bath block's south edge (the T&B/hall corner) and runs straight to the rear envelope. No 0.1 m offset between bath y_start values — the bath block reads as one clean horizontal stripe. 2026-07-20 update: this topology was restored as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld (which can't reach shallow-depth lots); the published minimum 11x9 needed both the kitchen floor AND the hall's greatest-dim RANGE loosened together -- neither alone was sufficient (confirmed by bisection: kitchen alone at any floor down to 1.5 stayed infeasible; hall's max_least_dim_m loosened alone stayed infeasible; only hall's min_greatest_dim_m specifically, down to <=2.6, combined with kitchen<=1.6, unlocks 11x9). Bath caps were NOT touched -- confirmed unnecessary via the same bisection.

notes: RESTORED 2026-07-20 as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld
(deleted the same day, then restored the same day once testing showed the LDK split
genuinely can't reach shallow-depth lots: its living/dining/kitchen stack needs at
least ~5.5 m buildable depth, confirmed via isolated-column bisection). This gr
(combined great_room, 2-deep public stack: great+kitchen) is the one to use down to
~5 m buildable depth -- verified feasible at 11x9 (buildable 7x5). cl_ld's
fallback_topology now points here (was dangling at a file deleted 2026-06-25, fixed
the same day this topology was restored). No fallback_below_buildable_sqm gate set --
the general infeasibility-triggered fallback (not the skip-straight-to-fallback area
gate) is deliberately what routes shallow-depth briefs here, since the actual binding
constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA (confirmed: widening a shallow
lot does NOT unlock cl_ld -- 12x9, 13x9, and 15x9 are all still infeasible on cl_ld
despite far more area than the 12x10 minimum that does work -- so an area-based skip-
ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot).

Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED at the REAR. First wide-shell with rear-clustered baths AND a hallway-served bedroom wing. Distinct from 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (single rear-bath, no ensuite, hall-served) and its own since-restored ds_gr-style siblings (mid-band common bath, no ensuite, no hall).
Floor band: this topology now covers the shallow/compact end (~50-60 sqm buildable,
11x9 through roughly 14x10) -- cl_ld picks up 12x10 and deeper. Originally designed
against 14x10 m as its own low edge before cl_ld existed; both topologies solve there,
so either is a valid choice at that size.
Larger lots (14x12, 15x11, 16x11) let master and bedrooms breathe toward the preferred high.
Layout intent (CANONICAL form — kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT, matches squarish catalog convention; brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):
Rear band  (1.5 m deep):    [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN       ]
Middle band (1.5 m deep):   [(master | HALL       | (great cont.) ]
Front band (3-4 m deep):    [ MASTER | STANDARD   | GREAT ROOM    ]
Three vertical sub-columns (canonical, kitchen-side right):
- LEFT  (3 m wide): [master front+middle, ensuite rear]
- MID   (3 m wide): [standard front, hall middle, common T&B rear]
- RIGHT (4 m wide): [great_room front, kitchen rear] — the LDK stack
Hall is a 4-walled circulation node:
- NORTH (rear): door to common T&B (bath's only door)
- SOUTH (front): door to standard bedroom (standard's only door)
- EAST: open mouth into great_room (the public connection — open-plan)
- WEST: door to master bedroom (master's only public door)

Master ensuite is master's exclusive (door on master's north/rear wall into ensuite). Master accesses public space ONLY via the hall — there is no direct master↔great door. This adds a small acoustic buffer between the master and the LDK, and keeps the master's west+south+north walls available for closets, windows, and the ensuite door.

Wet-stack cluster: ensuite's east = common's west (wet_core 1.2 m), and common's east = kitchen's west (wet_core 1.0 m). All three rooms (ensuite, common, kitchen) sit on the rear band, sharing one continuous plumbing chase along the rear elevation. The dirty kitchen in the rear setback completes the wet-utility row.

Bedroom widths: match_bedroom_widths=true forces master.width = standard.width. Bath widths: match_bath_widths=true forces common.width = ensuite.width. Combined with the left-anchor on master/ensuite (left wall) and the right-anchor on great/kitchen (right wall), this naturally tiles the bedroom wing into two 3 m sub-columns when the buildable width allows it.
Hall depth: lot_adjustment_profile floors the hallway at 1.4 m on compact lots so the hall↔great open mouth (which equals great's depth that extends into the hall's y-range) stays at 1.0 m minimum.

Carport: not declared in the base topology — this is the NCP form. To add a left-side carport (mirrors the canonical), set brief.carport_side='left' — the runner mirrors the topology and LDK appears on the left. For a right-side carport, add a building_void at rear_right but be aware the rear-right corner is the kitchen+dirty-kitchen wet stack so a right-side carport doesn't fit naturally here. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m.

Fallback: on shells too tight for the rear-bath cluster (envelope < ~55 sqm buildable or ratio outside 1.30-1.85), the runner falls back to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_gr (single common bath, no ensuite, no hall, simpler floor demand).
